description: HRV3C_site — LEVLFQGP, the 8-residue recognition/cleavage site for Human Rhinovirus 3C protease (EC 3.4.22.28); the protease cleaves between Q and G (LEVLFQ|GP), and is widely used to remove affinity or solubility tags from recombinant fusion proteins at 4 °C with high specificity and efficiency.

references.authors: Cordingley MG, Callahan PL, Sardana VV, Garsky VM, Colonno RJ
references.title: Substrate requirements of human rhinovirus 3C protease for peptide cleavage in vitro.

references.authors: Fan X, Li X, Zhou Y, Mei M, Liu P, Zhao J, Peng W, Jiang ZB, Yang S, Iverson BL, Zhang G, Yi L
references.title: Quantitative Analysis of the Substrate Specificity of Human Rhinovirus 3C Protease and Exploration of Its Substrate Recognition Mechanisms.
NT*-HRV3CP: An optimized construct of human rhinovirus 14 3C protease for high-yield expression and fast affinity-tag cleavage.
An overview of enzymatic reagents for the removal of affinity tags.

references.authors: Mei M, Fan X, Zhou Y, Zhang F, Zhang G, Yi L
references.title: A combinatorial strategy for HRV 3C protease engineering to achieve the N-terminal free cleavage.

references.authors: Xu J, Nakanishi T, Kato T, Park EY
references.title: In vivo enzymatic digestion of HRV 3C protease cleavage sites-containing proteins produced in a silkworm-baculovirus expression system.

references.authors: Xu H, Wang Q, Zhang Z, Yi L, Ma L, Zhai C
references.title: A simplified method to remove fusion tags from a xylanase of Bacillus sp. HBP8 with HRV 3C protease.

references.authors: Davis GJ, Wang QM, Cox GA, Johnson RB, Wakulchik M, Dotson CA, Villarreal EC
references.title: Expression and purification of recombinant rhinovirus 14 3CD proteinase and its comparison to the 3C proteinase.

label: HRV3C_site
note: Human rhinovirus 3C protease recognition and cleavage site (LEVLFQGP; Leu-Glu-Val-Leu-Phe-Gln-Gly-Pro). The 3C cysteine protease cleaves the peptide bond between Q (P1) and G (P1'), i.e. LEVLFQ|GP, leaving a Gly-Pro N-terminal scar on the downstream protein. The site derives from the natural HRV polyprotein processing sequence at residues 1423-1430 of HRV3 (UniProt Q82081). It is the recognition sequence used in the PreScission protease reagent (a GST-HRV3C fusion). Source-verified to UniProt Q82081 (POLG_HRV3) at 100% identity over all 8 aa. No functional sub-features are present: this is a single atomic cleavage-site element.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt Q82081 (POLG_HRV3, Human rhinovirus 3 genome polyprotein, Swiss-Prot reviewed), residues 1423-1430 (LEVLFQGP); cleavage between Q1428 and G1429 annotated as "Cleavage; by protease 3C"; 100% identity over 8/8 aa, verified by direct UniProt REST fetch.

functional_claims.label: LEVLFQGP is the HRV 3C protease recognition and cleavage site; the protease cleaves between Q (P1) and G (P1'), releasing the downstream protein with a Gly-Pro N-terminal scar

value.protease_synonym: HRV3C protease, HRV 3C protease, PreScission protease (as fusion)

source.quote: Substrate requirements of human rhinovirus 3C protease for peptide cleavage in vitro — minimum substrate TLFQ/GP; P1=Gln and scissile bond Q|G established experimentally
source.section: title and results; substrate truncation/substitution experiments

functional_claims.label: HRV 3C protease has dominant specificity at P1 (Gln > Glu) and P1' (Gly/Ala/Cys/Ser); P4 (hydrophobic) and P2 (Leu/Phe) also contribute to recognition

value.extended_recognition_note: P1=Gln and P1'=Gly are the strongest determinants; S1 pocket residue His160 and S1' pocket residue Thr141 identified structurally
value.improved_substrate: LEVLFQ|GM (P1'=Met reported as improved at elevated temperatures)

source.quote: P1=Gln/Glu and P1'=Gly/Ala/Cys/Ser as dominant determinants; LEVLFQ↓GM as improved substrate at high temperature
source.section: YESS substrate profiling results and discussion

functional_claims.label: LEVLFQGP derives from the natural HRV3 polyprotein processing sequence (residues 1423-1430, UniProt Q82081); the 3C protease cleaves this site during viral polyprotein maturation

value.origin_protein: Human rhinovirus 3 genome polyprotein (UniProt Q82081, POLG_HRV3)
value.annotated_feature: Cleavage; by protease 3C between Q1428 and G1429
value.biological_context: Viral polyprotein processing during HRV3 replication

source.quote: Substrate requirements of human rhinovirus 3C protease for peptide cleavage in vitro — sites in the viral polyprotein define the recognition consensus

functional_claims.label: HRV 3C protease cleaves LEVLFQGP efficiently at 4 °C, enabling tag removal under conditions that minimize target protein degradation or aggregation

value.advantage: active at low temperature; minimizes proteolytic damage to target protein during extended incubation
value.comparison: more active at 4 °C than thrombin or Factor Xa under comparable conditions

source.quote: demonstrates 4 °C cleavage in minutes; confirms LEVLFQ/GX as the stringent recognition sequence; describes PreScission protease as GST-HRV3C fusion
source.section: results and discussion; optimized protease construct for rapid tag cleavage

functional_claims.label: Cleavage of LEVLFQGP leaves a Gly-Pro (GP) dipeptide scar on the N-terminus of the released downstream protein; this scar may be undesirable for structural or functional studies requiring a native N-terminus

value.limitation: GP scar retained on downstream protein N-terminus after cleavage
mitigation_strategies: use engineered 3C variants with expanded P1' specificity (e.g. C3 variant) to reduce scar
alternative: incorporate site downstream of TEV if scar-free cleavage is needed

source.quote: Documents the GP scar problem; reports engineered C3 variant with expanded P1' specificity; confirms LEVLFQ↓GP and LEVLFQ↓GM as canonical and improved substrates respectively

functional_claims.label: HRV 3C protease shows very high cleavage specificity for LEVLFQGP compared to thrombin and Factor Xa; off-target cleavage within the fusion partner is rare

value.specificity: high relative to thrombin and Factor Xa
value.context: in vitro cleavage after affinity purification of fusion protein

source.quote: Review discusses LEVLFQGP as a standard cleavage site, its comparison to TEV protease, 4 °C activity advantage, and notes the sequence may have been refined from natural ETLFQGP; also documents GP scar and PreScission protease variants
source.section: HRV 3C protease section; comparison to other proteases

functional_claims.label: LEVLFQGP is functional as a tag-removal site across diverse expression systems including bacterial and insect cell (baculovirus) expression, whether cleaved in vitro or in vivo

value.in_vivo_efficiency: high; documented for BEVS-expressed fusion proteins

source.quote: Demonstrates that LEVLFQGP site is used in diverse expression systems (E. coli and insect BEVS); site cleaved in vivo with high efficiency

functional_claims.label: The LEVLFQGM variant (P1'=Met instead of Gly) is identified as an improved substrate at elevated temperatures in quantitative specificity profiling

value.effect: improved cleavage efficiency at elevated temperature
value.note: Scar would be MP rather than GP on the released downstream protein

source.quote: identifies LEVLFQ↓GM as improved substrate at high temperature
